economic_leverage.note: Economic debt (incl. lease liabilities) over plain EBITDA — no rent add-back to the denominator.

obs_items.label: Capacity purchase agreement with Republic Airways (equity-method related party)

citation.quote: In 2025, 2024 and 2023, we recognized $658 million, $612 million and $636 million, respectively, of expense under our capacity purchase agreement with Republic Airways Inc. (Republic). We hold a 20.8% equity interest in Republic Airways Holdings Inc. (Republic Holdings), the parent company of Republic.

amount.note: Republic is a related party (AAG holds a 20.8% equity interest in Republic Holdings). The $658 million is annual expense recognized under the capacity purchase agreement, not a disclosed total future contractual commitment balance, so it is informational; no fixed obligation amount is quantified in the provided text.

obs_items.label: AAG guarantee of American's obligations under Credit Facilities and Senior Secured Notes
obs_items.notes: AAG guarantees American's obligations under the Credit Facilities and the 7.25% and 8.50% Senior Secured Notes. These underlying obligations are already consolidated within AAG's reported debt, so the parent guarantee is not incremental economic debt at the consolidated level. No separate amount disclosed for the guarantee itself.

covenants.creditor_note: Agents/trustee as named in the agreement. Beneficial holders of loans and bonds are not public; registered-fund holdings (N-PORT) are a partial view shown separately when available.
covenants.guarantors: The Guaranteeing Subsidiaries — Madrid IP Lux GP S.à r.l., Madrid IP Lux Holdco SCS ("Madrid IP Lux 1"), and Madrid IP Lux Holdco 2 SCS ("Madrid IP Lux 2") — each joining as a Guarantor under the Indenture; original Guarantors as defined in the base Indenture are not reproduced in this text.
covenants.anchor_clause: "Each Guaranteeing Subsidiary hereby agrees to be a Guarantor under the Indenture and to be bound by the terms of the Indenture applicable to a Guarantor, including Article 10 thereof, as if it were an original signatory thereto."

lme_vectors.rationale: This supplemental indenture is a guarantee-joinder only and contains no lien, priming, or sacred-rights provisions.
lme_vectors.basis: The document's operative clauses cover only agreement to guarantee, execution, governing law, and ratification; substantive covenants reside in the base Indenture not reproduced here.
lme_vectors.quote: Each Guaranteeing Subsidiary hereby agrees to be a Guarantor under the Indenture and to be bound by the terms of the Indenture applicable to a Guarantor, including Article 10 thereof

lme_vectors.rationale: No unrestricted-subsidiary or IP/asset-transfer provisions appear; the text only adds three Luxembourg IP entities as guarantors.
lme_vectors.basis: Parties include 'Madrid IP Lux' entities and 'AAdvantage Loyalty IP Ltd.,' but the supplement contains no transfer or designation mechanics.
lme_vectors.quote: together with Madrid IP Lux GP and Madrid IP Lux 1, the “ Guaranteeing Subsidiaries ”

lme_vectors.rationale: The supplement references the existing Notes but establishes no debt-incurrence capacity or ratios.
lme_vectors.basis: Recitals describe the already-issued $3.5bn 5.500% 2026 Notes and $3.0bn 5.750% 2029 Notes; no incurrence covenant is set out.
lme_vectors.quote: providing for the initial issuance of $3,500,000,000 of 5.500% Senior Secured Notes due 2026 ... and $3,000,000,000 of 5.750% Senior Secured Notes due 2029

lme_vectors.rationale: No restricted-payments covenant or basket is contained in this guarantee-joinder supplement.
lme_vectors.basis: Operative provisions are limited to the guarantee agreement and boilerplate; RP mechanics are in the base Indenture, not this text.
lme_vectors.quote: Each Guaranteeing Subsidiary agrees that the Guarantee shall remain in full force and effect

citation.quote: "Each Guaranteeing Subsidiary hereby agrees to be a Guarantor under the Indenture and to be bound by the terms of the Indenture applicable to a Guarantor, including Article 10 thereof, as if it were an original signatory thereto."

baskets.name: 2025 Incremental Term Loans (this amendment)
baskets.quote: the Designated 2025 Incremental Term Lender hereby agree to make the 2025 Incremental Term Loans to the Borrowers on the Third Amendment Effective Date in the aggregate principal amount of $1,000,000,000.

baskets.name: Incremental Term Loans capacity (ratio-based, LTV governed)
baskets.value: Unlimited subject to pro forma LTV Ratio no more than 55%
baskets.quote: the pro forma LTV Ratio, immediately after giving effect to the making of the Incremental Term Loans, shall be no more than 55%

baskets.name: Permitted First Lien Notes / secured debt
baskets.value: Subject to pro forma LTV Ratio not more than 55%
baskets.quote: after giving effect to such indebtedness, the pro forma LTV Ratio shall not be more than 55%

baskets.name: Retained Agreements not transferred to Collateral (leakage limit)
baskets.value: less than 15.0% of AAdvantage Revenue on a pro forma basis
baskets.quote: the aggregate amount of payments in cash produced by the Retained Agreements not so transferred is less than 15.0% of the AAdvantage Revenue in such period (on a pro forma basis)

covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Six months after the Third Amendment Effective Date (MFN applies to Incremental Term Loans incurred prior to that date)
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.06 governs Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries; specific mechanics not reproduced in the provided excerpt. The AAdvantage IP/loyalty collateral structure is ring-fenced in SPV Parties and subject to the AAdvantage Agreement transfer/re-designation regime (Retained Agreements capped at less than 15.0% of AAdvantage Revenue on a pro forma basis).
covenants.anchor_clause: (a) the pro forma LTV Ratio, immediately after giving effect to the making of the Incremental Term Loans, shall be no more than 55%; ... (g) the All-In Yield applicable to any Incremental Term Loans shall be determined by the Borrowers and the applicable Lenders providing such Incremental Term Loans; provided, that if the All-In Yield of any such Incremental Term Loans that are incurred prior to the date that is six months after the Third Amendment Effective Date exceeds the All-In Yield on any then-outstanding 2025 Incremental Term Loans ... by more than 0.50%, the applicable margins applicable to such outstanding 2025 Incremental Term Loans shall be increased to the extent necessary so that the yield on such 2025 Incremental Term Loans is 0.50% less than the All-In Yield on such Incremental Term Loans.

lme_vectors.rationale: Incremental Term Loans and Permitted First Lien Notes may be incurred without a hard dollar cap, subject only to a pro forma LTV Ratio of no more than 55%, no default, and MFN/maturity conditions.
lme_vectors.basis: Annex A Specified Terms condition (a) sets a 55% LTV incurrence test; Negative Covenants section likewise permits secured debt subject to 55% pro forma LTV; this amendment adds $1.0bn of incremental term loans.
lme_vectors.quote: the pro forma LTV Ratio, immediately after giving effect to the making of the Incremental Term Loans, shall be no more than 55%

lme_vectors.rationale: The amendment relies on Required Lenders consent under Section 10.08 for the Specified Amendment and pre-wires consent of the Designated 2025 Incremental Term Lender, but the sacred-rights / voting provisions of Section 10.08 are not reproduced, so priming protection cannot be confirmed.
lme_vectors.basis: Effectiveness of the Specified Amendment is tied to Required Lenders consent 'in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.08'; the substance of Section 10.08 is not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: the Required Lenders (and taking into account the consent of the Designated 2025 Incremental Term Lender and its assignees provided in the foregoing clause (a)) consenting to the terms of the Specified Terms ... in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.08

lme_vectors.rationale: The structure ring-fences AAdvantage IP in SPV Parties and requires transferred Retained Agreements to be pledged as first-lien Collateral, with leakage of unpledged Retained Agreements capped below 15% of AAdvantage Revenue, constraining value transfer away from lenders.
lme_vectors.basis: Retained Agreement transfer/re-designation regime caps unpledged cash flows at <15.0% of AAdvantage Revenue; transferred agreements 'shall be pledged as Collateral on a first lien basis'; dedicated IP Contribution Structure and SPV covenants (5.06-5.13).
lme_vectors.quote: Any Retained Agreements so transferred to the Borrower shall be pledged as Collateral on a first lien basis and shall thereafter be an AAdvantage Agreement

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 6.01 Restricted Payments exists but its baskets and thresholds are not reproduced in the provided excerpt, so restricted-payment leakage capacity cannot be assessed from the text.
lme_vectors.basis: Table of contents lists Section 6.01 Restricted Payments (p.151) but the operative RP baskets are not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: Section 6.01 Restricted Payments 151

citation.quote: (a) the pro forma LTV Ratio, immediately after giving effect to the making of the Incremental Term Loans, shall be no more than 55%; ... (g) the All-In Yield applicable to any Incremental Term Loans shall be determined by the Borrowers and the applicable Lenders providing such Incremental Term Loans; provided, that if the All-In Yield of any such Incremental Term Loans that are incurred prior to the date that is six months after the Third Amendment Effective Date exceeds the All-In Yield on any then-outstanding 2025 Incremental Term Loans ... by more than 0.50%, the applicable margins applicable to such outstanding 2025 Incremental Term Loans shall be increased to the extent necessary so that the yield on such 2025 Incremental Term Loans is 0.50% less than the All-In Yield on such Incremental Term Loans.

lme_vectors.basis: No definitions or covenants concerning unrestricted subsidiaries or asset transfers appear in the excerpted amendment.
lme_vectors.quote: each Revolving Lender that is a party to the Credit Agreement immediately prior to the Fourth Amendment

lme_vectors.rationale: The amendment extends existing revolving commitments and adds new revolving lenders' commitments as a maturity extension, but does not set out incremental facility capacity, ratio debt, or MFN terms.
lme_vectors.basis: New Revolving Lenders (HSBC, MUFG, BoT) join in connection with a Revolver Extension Offer under Section 2.28(b); no incremental sizing or ratio tests are quoted.
lme_vectors.quote: HSBC Bank USA, N.A. ... MUFG Union Bank, N.A. ... and BOKF, NA dba Bank of Texas ... collectively, the "New Revolving Lenders"

lme_vectors.rationale: The excerpt contains no restricted-payment, dividend, or investment covenant language; it addresses fees, conditions to effectiveness, and titles/roles only.
lme_vectors.basis: Provisions cover the Upfront Fee, Commitment Fee, expense reimbursement under Section 10.04, and agent designations only.
lme_vectors.quote: the Borrower shall have paid (or caused to be paid), for the account of each Extending Revolving Lender, a one time, non-refundable fee

citation.quote: "the Borrower may make a Revolver Extension Offer to all Revolving Lenders holding Revolving Commitments under the Credit Agreement to extend the maturity date of each such Revolving Lender's Revolving Commitment" (Section 2.28(b) of the Credit Agreement, as referenced in the Fourth Amendment).

covenants.admin_agent: Citibank, N.A. (administrative agent under the 2023 Credit Agreement)
covenants.trustee: URE Dated as of December 4, 2023 Wilmington Trust, National Association
covenants.collateral_agent: Wilmington Trust, National Association (as Collateral Trustee)
covenants.creditor_note: Agents/trustee as named in the agreement. Beneficial holders of loans and bonds are not public; registered-fund holdings (N-PORT) are a partial view shown separately when available.
covenants.guarantors: The Guarantors party hereto from time to time; the Note Guarantee is provided by the Parent (guaranteeing the Company's obligations) and any other person providing a Guarantee pursuant to Section 9.01(i)

financial_covenants.test_frequency: As of any date of determination (see Section 4.14; appraisals delivered under Section 4.13)
financial_covenants.quote: Collateral Coverage Deficiency means, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.

baskets.name: Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio (ratio debt incurrence test)
baskets.value: Used as the ratio-based incurrence test under Section 4.08; specific required ratio not shown in provided excerpt
baskets.quote: Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio means with respect to any specified Person for any specified period, the ratio of the Consolidated EBITDAR of such Person for such period to the Fixed Charges of such Person for such period.

baskets.name: Permitted Disposition (de minimis Collateral disposal basket)
baskets.value: Less than $50,000,000 aggregate Fair Market Value during any six-month period
baskets.quote: one or more transactions that involves the Disposition of assets having a Fair Market Value in aggregate of less than $50,000,000 during any six-month period

baskets.name: Material Indebtedness threshold
baskets.value: $150,000,000 (principal outstanding under same agreement, other than the Notes)
baskets.quote: Material Indebtedness means Indebtedness of the Company and/or Restricted Subsidiaries (other than the Notes) outstanding under the same agreement in a principal amount exceeding $150,000,000.

ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary losses and net losses on Disposition of assets
Provision for income/profit taxes
Fixed Charges (interest expense, finance lease interest component, guaranteed/secured interest, preferred dividends grossed-up, cash aircraft rent expense)
Foreign currency translation losses (including on debt remeasurement)
Depreciation, amortization (including intangibles) and other non-cash charges
Amortization of debt discount
Non-cash equity-based compensation grants to employees
Net loss on sale/disposition of capital assets
Losses under fuel hedging arrangements
Business interruption insurance proceeds

Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers (AMR Merger, Airlines Merger), dispositions, debt incurrence, equity issuances or investments
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 4.11 governs designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries. The text contemplates Unrestricted Subsidiaries including Non-Recourse Financing Subsidiaries; Non-Recourse Debt holders may have recourse to the Equity Interests of an Unrestricted Subsidiary, and Consolidated Net Income excludes the net income/loss of any Unrestricted Subsidiary. Full designation mechanics/conditions in Section 4.11 are not reproduced in the provided text.
covenants.anchor_clause: Collateral Coverage Deficiency means, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.

lme_vectors.rationale: Collateral is governed by a Collateral Trust Agreement with a Pari Passu Lien Debt regime allowing future secured debt to share ratably on a first-lien basis, but the provided text does not reproduce the sacred-rights/voting or lien-subordination protections needed to assess priming risk.
lme_vectors.basis: Pari Passu Lien Debt definition permits new first-lien debt secured equally and ratably with the Notes via a Collateral Trust Joinder; Junior Lien Debt regime also exists. Amendment/sacred-rights provisions not in excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: Indebtedness ... that is secured equally and ratably with the Notes and the 2023 Credit Agreement on a first-lien basis (with respect to the Collateral) by a Pari Passu Lien that is permitted to be incurred and so secured under each then extant Secured Debt Document

lme_vectors.rationale: The indenture permits Unrestricted Subsidiaries and Non-Recourse Financing Subsidiaries to which assets could migrate, but the provided text does not include an explicit IP/collateral-transfer blocker or the Section 4.11 designation limits and Section 4.07/4.15 asset-transfer restrictions needed to assess dropdown risk.
lme_vectors.basis: Existence of Unrestricted Subsidiary and Non-Recourse Financing Subsidiary concepts; Section 4.15 governs Dispositions and Release of Collateral (not reproduced); Section 4.11 designation mechanics not reproduced.
lme_vectors.quote: Non-Recourse Financing Subsidiary means any Unrestricted Subsidiary that (a) has no Indebtedness other than Non-Recourse Debt and (b) engages in no activities other than those relating to the financing of specified assets and other activities incidental thereto.

lme_vectors.rationale: The indenture expressly allows incurrence of additional Pari Passu Lien Debt sharing first-lien collateral ratably with the Notes, and Additional Notes plus Junior Lien Debt, subject to Section 4.08 and collateral-trust designation, indicating meaningful capacity for additional secured debt.
lme_vectors.basis: Additional Notes permitted under Section 2.02 subject to Section 4.08; Pari Passu Lien Debt and Junior Lien Debt definitions permit additional secured debt sharing the Collateral; incurrence tested via Fixed Charge Coverage Ratio / Permitted Debt.
lme_vectors.quote: Indebtedness represented by notes issued by the Company under this Indenture or any other indenture, Indebtedness under any Pari Passu Credit Agreement or other Indebtedness ... that is secured equally and ratably with the Notes and the 2023 Credit Agreement on a first-lien basis

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 4.07 (Restricted Payments) exists and defined terms (Excluded Contributions, builder-basket references to Section 4.07(a)(2)) suggest a builder basket, but the operative RP baskets, thresholds and carve-outs are not reproduced in the provided text.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 4.07 Restricted Payments referenced; Excluded Contributions feed into Section 4.07(a)(2); Investments and Disqualified Stock definitions tie into RP capacity. Actual RP builder-basket figures not in excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: Excluded Contributions will not be considered to be net proceeds of Qualifying Equity Interests for purposes of Section 4.07(a)(2) hereof.

citation.quote: Collateral Coverage Deficiency means, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.
